com.pax.poslink.peripheries.tech

Class MifareClassic

  • java.lang.Object
    • BasicTechnology
      • com.pax.poslink.peripheries.tech.MifareClassic

  • public class MifareClassic
extends BasicTechnology
    Provides access to MIFARE Classic properties and I/O operations on a CardInfo. Support to control remote card reader.

    Acquire a MifareClassic object using get(com.pax.poslink.peripheries.tech.CardInfo).

    MIFARE Classic is also known as MIFARE Standard.

    MIFARE Classic tags are divided into sectors, and each sector is sub-divided into blocks. Block size is always 16 bytes (BLOCK_SIZE. Sector size varies.

    • MIFARE Classic Mini are 320 bytes (SIZE_MINI), with 5 sectors each of 4 blocks.
    • MIFARE Classic 1k are 1024 bytes (SIZE_1K), with 16 sectors each of 4 blocks.
    • MIFARE Classic 2k are 2048 bytes (SIZE_2K), with 32 sectors each of 4 blocks.
    • MIFARE Classic 4k} are 4096 bytes (SIZE_4K). The first 32 sectors contain 4 blocks and the last 8 sectors contain 16 blocks.

    MIFARE Classic require authentication on a per-sector basis before any other I/O operations on that sector can be performed. There are two keys per sector, and ACL bits determine what I/O operations are allowed on that sector after authenticating with a key. authenticateSectorWithKeyA(int, byte[]) and authenticateSectorWithKeyB(int, byte[]).

    Note: Methods that perform I/O operations require the "com.pax.permission.PICC" permission.

    Since:
    V1.04.00

    • Method Detail

      • get

        public static MifareClassic get(CardInfo cardInfo)
        Get an instance of MifareClassic for the given cardInfo.

        If the card is not a MifareClassic, it will returns null.

        Parameters:
        cardInfo - the card info
        Returns:
        MIFARE Classic object

      • get

        public static MifareClassic get(Context context,
                                MifareClassic.RemoteParam remoteParam)
        Get an instance of MifareClassic for the CommSetting. Use CommSetting to create an instance, need to setRemoteParam(RemoteParam).
        Parameters:
        context - App context
        RemoteParam - the remoteParam
        Returns:
        MIFARE Classic object

      • authenticateSectorWithKeyA

        public boolean authenticateSectorWithKeyA(int sectorIndex,
                                          byte[] key)
        Authenticate a sector with key A.

        Successful authentication of a sector with key A enables other I/O operations on that sector. The set of operations granted by key A key depends on the ACL bits set in that sector. For more information see the MIFARE Classic specification on http://www.nxp.com.

        A failed authentication attempt will cause the card to lose its activation state, so authentication to other sectors will be lost.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Parameters:
        sectorIndex - index of sector to authenticate, starting from 0
        key - 6-byte authentication key
        Returns:
        true on success, false on authentication failure

      • authenticateSectorWithKeyB

        public boolean authenticateSectorWithKeyB(int sectorIndex,
                                          byte[] key)
        Authenticate a sector with key B.

        Successful authentication of a sector with key B enables other I/O operations on that sector. The set of operations granted by key B key depends on the ACL bits set in that sector. For more information see the MIFARE Classic specification on http://www.nxp.com.

        A failed authentication attempt will cause the card to lose its activation state, so authentication to other sectors will be lost.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Parameters:
        sectorIndex - index of sector to authenticate, starting from 0
        key - 6-byte authentication key
        Returns:
        true on success, false on authentication failure

      • operate

        public void operate(int blockIndex,
                    int value,
                    int updateBlockNo,
                    MifareClassic.OperationCommand m1OperationCommand)
             throws PiccException
        Perform increment/decrement/backup operation on the designated data block blockIndex of the M1 card, and update the value after the operation to another designated data block updateBlockNo.
        • If the blockIndex and updateBlockNo block numbers are the same, if MifareClassic.OperationCommand.OPERATION_INCREASE or MifareClassic.OperationCommand.OPERATION_DECREASE operation is performed, the block number will be incremented or decremented.
        • If the block numbers of blockIndex and updateBlockNo are different, if the MifareClassic.OperationCommand.OPERATION_INCREASE or MifareClassic.OperationCommand.OPERATION_DECREASE operation is performed, the amount stored in blockIndex will not change, and the amount of updateBlockNo will be equal to the amount of blockIndex for corresponding increase or decrease.
        • If MifareClassic.OperationCommand.OPERATION_BACKUP operation is performed, value will not work, and the amount in blockIndex will be stored in updateBlockNo.

        The block number involved in this function must be a numeric block, and blockIndex and updateBlockNo must be in the same sector.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Parameters:
        blockIndex - index of block to operate, starting from 0
        value - The amount of increment or decrement
        updateBlockNo - Specify the block number to which the operation result is finally written
        m1OperationCommand - OperationCommand
        Throws:
        PiccException

      • readBlock

        public byte[] readBlock(int blockIndex)
                 throws PiccException
        Read 16-byte block.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Parameters:
        blockIndex - index of block to read, starting from 0
        Returns:
        16 byte block
        Throws:
        PiccException

      • writeBlock

        public void writeBlock(int blockIndex,
                       byte[] data)
                throws PiccException
        Write 16-byte block.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Parameters:
        blockIndex - index of block to write, starting from 0
        data - 16 bytes of data to write
        Throws:
        PiccException

      • setRemoteParam

        public void setRemoteParam(MifareClassic.RemoteParam remoteParam)
        Set up RemoteParam. Only applicable when creating an instance using CommSetting.
        Parameters:
        remoteParam - RemoteParams

      • transceive

        public byte[] transceive(byte[] data)
                  throws PiccException
        Send raw data to the card and receive the response.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Parameters:
        data - bytes to send
        Returns:
        bytes received in response
        Throws:
        PiccException

      • transceive

        public byte[] transceive(byte[] data,
                         byte mode)
                  throws PiccException
        Send raw data to the card and receive the response.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Mode:
        Bit0 = 0, means sending disabled CRC, Bit0 = 1, means sending enabled CRC.
        Bit1 = 0, means that the CRC is disabled for reception, Bit0 = 1, means that the CRC is enabled for reception.
        Bit2 ~ Bit7 will be used in the future, currently set to 0

        Parameters:
        data - bytes to send
        mode - mode of transceive
        Returns:
        bytes received in response
        Throws:
        PiccException

      • getBlockCountInSector

        public int getBlockCountInSector(int sectorIndex)
        Return the number of blocks in the given sector.

        Does not cause any RF activity and does not block.

        Parameters:
        sectorIndex - index of sector, starting from 0
        Returns:
        number of blocks in the sector

      • getType

        public int getType()
        Return the type of this MIFARE Classic compatible tag.

        One of TYPE_UNKNOWN, TYPE_CLASSIC, TYPE_PLUS or TYPE_PRO.

        Does not cause any RF activity and does not block.

        Returns:
        type

      • getSize

        public int getSize()
        Return the size of the tag in bytes

        One of SIZE_MINI, SIZE_1K, SIZE_2K, SIZE_4K. These constants are equal to their respective size in bytes.

        Does not cause any RF activity and does not block.

        Returns:
        size in bytes

      • getBlockCount

        public int getBlockCount()
        Return the total number of MIFARE Classic blocks.

        Does not cause any RF activity and does not block.

        Returns:
        total number of blocks

      • getSectorCount

        public int getSectorCount()
        Return the number of MIFARE Classic sectors.

        Does not cause any RF activity and does not block.

        Returns:
        number of sectors

      • blockToSector

        public int blockToSector(int blockIndex)
        Return the sector that contains a given block.

        Does not cause any RF activity and does not block.

        Parameters:
        blockIndex - index of block to lookup, starting from 0
        Returns:
        sector index that contains the block

      • sectorToBlock

        public int sectorToBlock(int sectorIndex)
        Return the first block of a given sector.

        Does not cause any RF activity and does not block.

        Parameters:
        sectorIndex - index of sector to lookup, starting from 0
        Returns:
        block index of first block in sector
